The family Rubiaceae includes over 13,000 species of flowering plants, and Afrocanthium pseudorandii is one of them. intro: Afrocanthium pseudorandii is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and inflammatory diseases, due to its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Afrocanthium pseudorandii include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
